title: Knee / Hip Osteoarthritis Kellgren-Lawrence Grading (PWDR)
sub_domain: Joint-space-narrowing + osteophyte recovery from radiograph with KL-grade categorical readout
source_data_quality_note: v2 PWDR Principle. Wraps L1-031 X-ray radiography with deterministic Kellgren-Lawrence 1957 grading rules to assign osteoarthritis severity {KL-0 normal, KL-1 doubtful, KL-2 minimal, KL-3 moderate, KL-4 severe}. Universal in orthopedic / rheumatology radiology. UTSW Orthopedic Surgery + Rheumatology programs.

gate_substitutions.conservation_substitute: softmax simplex closure on KL-grade ordinal {KL-0, KL-1, KL-2, KL-3, KL-4}

discrete_readout.threshold_function: Kellgren-Lawrence(radiograph) = step function on (joint_space_width_mm, osteophyte_count, subchondral_sclerosis, subchondral_cyst, deformity): KL-0 = normal joint; KL-1 = doubtful narrowing + possible osteophyte; KL-2 = definite osteophytes + possible JSN; KL-3 = multiple osteophytes + definite JSN + sclerosis + possible deformity; KL-4 = large osteophytes + marked JSN + severe sclerosis + definite deformity. Per Kellgren-Lawrence 1957 Atlas of Standard Radiographs of Arthritis.
discrete_readout.underlying_physical_state: joint-space width [mm], osteophyte morphology + count, subchondral bone density gradient, joint-line obliquity recovered from L1-031 weight-bearing radiograph (knee: AP standing + lateral; hip: AP pelvis)
discrete_readout.threshold_continuity_proof: Joint-space-width and osteophyte-count are continuous on bone-edge geometric reconstruction; KL-grade boundaries are step functions with measure-zero hyperplanes. Smoothed-grading globally Lipschitz with constant ~0.06 per percentage-point of feature-recovery error.

related_principles: L1-029 (CT — sibling cross-sectional bone modality, informational)
L1-030 (MRI — sibling cartilage assessment modality, informational)

E.description: Knee / Hip OA KL Grading (PWDR): wraps L1-031 with Kellgren-Lawrence 1957 grading rules. Stage 1 (analytical, from L1-031): from weight-bearing radiograph, recover joint-space width, osteophyte morphology, subchondral bone density, joint-line obliquity. Stage 2 (deterministic threshold): apply KL atlas decision tree; OARSI compartment grade overlay if requested. Difficulty tier delta = 3.
E.forward_model: Stage 1 (L1-031): radiograph -> {JSW, osteophyte_count, subchondral_sclerosis, deformity}. Stage 2: KL_grade = step(features at thresholds per Kellgren-Lawrence 1957 atlas).
E.world_state_x: Per-radiograph KL grade {0, 1, 2, 3, 4}; optionally per-compartment OARSI subgrades.
E.observation_y: Weight-bearing radiograph (knee: AP standing + lateral; hip: AP pelvis; typically 2500x3000 pixel digital radiograph).

G.dag: L.xray_source -> L.attenuation_projection -> L.joint_space_measurement -> L.osteophyte_detection -> L.kl_grade_classifier -> int.spatial
coupling_constraints: features_to_grade: joint-space and osteophyte features are jointly observed and jointly determine KL grade per the Kellgren-Lawrence atlas decision tree

W.regime: Existence inherited from L1-031. Uniqueness conditional on consistent weight-bearing positioning. Stability dominated by manual_grader_inter_rater_kappa (~0.55-0.75 typical). Joint Hadamard well-posedness established by Kellgren-Lawrence 1957 (foundational), Altman 1986 (ACR criteria), Tiulpin 2018 (deep learning KL grading benchmark), Norman 2019 (DeepKnee multi-task).

C.solver_class: linear-operator + image-segmentation [JSW measurement + osteophyte detection] + categorical-readout [KL atlas classifier] | end-to-end deep neural [DeepKnee, Tiulpin OA-CNN]
C.error_bound: Inherits L1-031. Misclassification rate ~10-20% in inter-rater studies; KL is known for high inter-rater variability (one grade adjacent agreement ~95%).
C.complexity: O(H * W) for stage 1; O(1) for stage 2

recast_attempted.recast_path: Original: 'Grade osteoarthritis severity from radiograph'. Recast: 'Recover JSW + osteophyte + sclerosis features via L1-031; apply KL 1957 atlas decision tree'. Feasible because OA features have well-defined geometric forward model.

joint_well_posedness_references: Kellgren JH, Lawrence JS (1957) Radiological assessment of osteoarthrosis. Ann Rheum Dis 16(4):494-502.
Altman R et al. (1986) Development of criteria for the classification and reporting of osteoarthritis. Classification of osteoarthritis of the knee. Arthritis Rheum 29(8):1039-1049.
Tiulpin A et al. (2018) Automatic knee osteoarthritis diagnosis from plain radiographs: a deep learning-based approach. Sci Rep 8:1727.

Norman B, Pedoia V, Noworolski A, Link TM, Majumdar S (2019) Applying densely connected convolutional neural networks for staging osteoarthritis severity from plain radiographs. J Digit Imaging 32(3):471-477.
Altman RD, Gold GE (2007) Atlas of individual radiographic features in osteoarthritis, revised. OARSI.

distinctness_audit.rationale: L1-031 recovers attenuation; L1-527 wraps with KL grading. Different solution_space (1D KL grade vs 2D attenuation); adds L.joint_space_measurement, L.osteophyte_detection, L.kl_grade_classifier primitives.

v3_metadata.clinical_context: Universal in orthopedic / rheumatology radiology. KL grading is the international standard for OA severity in clinical trials and routine surveillance. UTSW Orthopedic Surgery (knee/hip arthroplasty referral) + Rheumatology programs. Deep learning systems achieve 0.83 quadratic-weighted-kappa vs radiologist consensus on OAI dataset.
